mathematical_definition: Assigns a pseudo-random scalar to each integer lattice point and interpolates between neighbours using a C2-continuous quintic smoothstep. Cheaper than Perlin noise because no gradient vectors are needed — only a single hash per corner.
art_failure_modes: Blocky grid artefacts when interpolation degree is too low (linear) or frequency is very high.
Looks washed-out and low-contrast compared to Perlin/Simplex — needs contrast stretch or gamma.
FBM of value noise can produce noticeably banded horizontal/vertical features; rotate coordinates per octave to mitigate.
Hash collisions at very large coordinate values can create visible seams; clamp or modulo input space.

complexity_notes.notes: 4 hash evaluations per 2D sample vs 4 gradient evaluations for Perlin — roughly 30-40% cheaper per octave on GPU.
